notes: Reverted interactive CSV staging UI while retaining sequential payload iteration capabilities internally, mitigating potential DOM collision rendering bugs during payload generation.
Introduced a batch CSV staging modal to COTProxy outputs, allowing sequential submission and processing of multiple import files concurrently.
Redesigned the frontend file-input layer to aggregate distinct file uploads into a dynamic list interface where users can preview payloads and individually remove items before broadcast.
Added descriptive reference row logic to generated bulk-import CSV templates mapping data structures for users, with internal bypasses to skip accidental header submissions during payload uploads.
Fixed CoT generation issue where the "__video" element was appended outside the "detail" root in the XML, preventing video inputs from parsing correctly in ATAK/iTAK.
Changed "input type=url" to "type=text" for stream inputs to properly support non-HTTP schema validation natively (RTSP/SRT urls).
Removed accidental local SQLite database references (.db) and runtime IDE properties (.DS_Store) from source control indexing via gitignore.
Added "Associated Links" customizability to COTProxy transforms. Allows users to attach custom URLs formatted into the CoT <detail><link> output schema directly via the UI.

features: Added CoT pipeline self-healing: Paused connections automatically re-test in the background every 10 minutes.
Added generalized Connection Pause: Outputs timing out sequentially (3 times) are now paused to preserve pipeline performance.
Updated Outputs UI to generically reflect Connection Paused state for both invalid TLS and dropped TCP links.
fixes: Hardened manual Output test button: ensures forced manual UI tests unconditionally pause missing outputs natively syncing with the background cooldown threshold.

notes: Backend API: Updated the manual 'Test TLS Connection' route. Previously, explicitly running the test and receiving a TLS Handshake failure would just alert the UI without proactively applying the 'TLS Paused' state to the config, requiring a user to wait for the background worker to cycle and pause it. Front-end API output tests that encounter bad certificates now proactively and immediately pause the output.

Backend: Fixed a logic bug where TLS connections with bad certificates failed to invoke the 'TLS Paused' safeguard when the TAK server drops the connection using TCP RST (ConnectionResetError/EOFError) rather than an explicit SSLError. Explicit socket layer timeouts and connection refusals are now properly routed to bypass the pause system to ensure compatibility with temporary server updates and reboots.

Outputs: added a 'Show Category' toggle for ADSB Direct outputs (defaults off). When off, category and registration (r) fields are actively stripped from the outbound JSON stream to prevent downstream ATAK plugins from concatenating callsign fields (e.g., flight-flight-A1) and forcing clean marker labels.

CoT push TLS: only put outputs into 'TLS Paused' state on explicitly fatal TLS handshake errors (`ssl.SSLError`). Transient TCP connection errors or routing timeouts will clear and retry properly without requiring manual admin intervention via 'Test TLS connection'.
CoT pipeline performance: pre-parse aircraft dictionary fields locally loop iteration once per cycle (instead of per-output), eliminating ~150,000 float conversions during delta/filter stages.
CoTProxy query: query for delta hexes natively without iterating via dummy dictionary generation.
CoT XML: emit `<track speed="..."/>` in m/s. Ground speed from ADSBHub/tar1090 (`gs`) is knots; previously speed was passed through as knots, so TAK showed roughly 2× the real speed.

CoT sender: optimize per-output range filter by adding conservative lat/lon bounding-box reject before `_haversine_nm()` (reduces expensive distance checks on outputs where range limits exclude most aircraft).

CoT sender performance: speed up COTProxy transform lookup by using `hex IN (...)` first (lets SQLite use index on (output_id, hex)), with fallback to legacy `UPPER(TRIM(hex)) IN (...)` only when no matches found. Reduces transforms_ms on outputs like id=79.

CoT sender performance: reduce COTProxy transform DB work by loading transforms only for delta-changed hexes plus cached-but-not-TISB hexes (preserves inclusion + seen_hexes pruning semantics). Should cut transforms_ms on outputs like id=79 (high n_filtered, low n_to_send).
CoT phase timing: split TLS path into join_encode (per-chunk join+UTF-8), sendall, and encode_send (wall total); Health page copy updated.

CoT XML: optional fast string-template builder gated by COT_XML_USE_TEMPLATE (shared payload with ElementTree; parity-tested). ElementTree path now assigns raw text/attributes so TAK receives correct single escaping for &, etc. Config → Services: one CoT card save for chunk size + template toggle; GET/POST /api/settings/cot-push (replaces /api/settings/cot-send-chunk); docker-compose passes COT_XML_USE_TEMPLATE.
Config → Services: admin field for CoT TLS send chunk size (COT_SEND_CHUNK_MESSAGES); GET/POST /api/settings/cot-send-chunk; persists host .env and restarts taknet-dashboard.
CoT push: send TLS payload in chunks of complete messages (default 200 per sendall); env COT_SEND_CHUNK_MESSAGES (1–5000); same space-delimited wire format; docker-compose passes var from .env.

CoT push: load COTProxy transforms with CotTransformModel.get_for_hexes (batched IN on current aircraft hexes) instead of full get_all per output — same behavior, far less I/O for large transform tables.

CoT push TLS: on TCP/TLS connect failure set config cot_tls_paused and skip the output until cleared; Outputs modal adds Test TLS connection (POST /api/outputs/<id>/cot-test-tls), banner, and TLS paused list badge; successful test clears pause and persists cot_url when the test used form host/port; changing cot_url or uploading certs clears pause; OutputModel.merge_config helper.

System Health: CoT push phase timing card with toggle (persists settings cot_phase_timing_ui); lines buffer on the page, refreshed every 5s; API GET/POST /api/health/cot-timing (admin). models: get_setting/set_setting. COT_PHASE_TIMING env still forces timings and gunicorn logs; UI checkbox disabled when env is set.

CoT sender: optional COT_PHASE_TIMING env (1/true/yes/on) logs per-cycle phase milliseconds (outputs DB, HTTP+JSON, per-output filter/transforms/build_loop, cert, TLS connect, encode+send, buf_bytes) to gunicorn.error for bottleneck analysis.
docker-compose: pass COT_PHASE_TIMING into the dashboard service from .env.

CoT push outputs: TLS only in the UI (remove plain TCP); server address field strips http/https (and tcp/tls) prefixes on blur and when saving; backend parses tls:// endpoints and rejects tcp://; cot_pipeline normalizes mistaken nested schemes in stored cot_url.

Revert feeder tunnel proxy (web/routes/feeder_tunnel.py) to pre–FR24-settings work state (git 3f5c55f): removes auxiliary path normalization, bare-path 404 retries, base-href filename stripping, FR24/PiAware HTML service-prefix rewrites, and related routing helpers added for settings.html and root assets.

Tunnel FR24/PiAware HTML: do not apply double-rewrite skip heuristic when aux_service_prefix is set — FR24 pages repeat feeder paths in JSON and previously skipped <base> and href rewrites, breaking css/js loads.

Tunnel FR24/PiAware HTML rewrite fix: scope root-absolute links (src/href/url/action) to service path (/fr24 or /piaware) based on effective upstream path, preventing settings pages from requesting assets at /feeder/<id>/settings.html/* or /feeder/<id>/*.
Tunnel HTML <base href> fix: use document directory (strip filename like settings.html) so relative FR24 assets (fr24.css, jquery.js) resolve under /feeder/<id>/fr24/ instead of .../settings.html/.

Tunnel FR24 asset fix: treat /fr24/ as path prefix only (not /fr24.css filename); normalize /fr24.css and /jquery.js to /fr24/... for feeder nginx; extend 404 retry to bare root .css/.js; fix aux_ui_path detection.

Tunnel fallback for FR24/PiAware root HTML links: when bare /<name>.html requests return 404 (e.g. /settings.html), retry through feeder as /fr24/<name>.html then /piaware/<name>.html on dashboard target.

Tunnel FR24/PiAware navigation fix: normalize root-relative requests from /fr24/ and /piaware/ referers to service-prefixed feeder paths (e.g. /settings.html -> /fr24/settings.html) to prevent 404s on auxiliary UI pages.

Tunnel routing fix for PiAware/FR24 root-level assets: when Referer is a /piaware or /fr24 tunnel page, route ambiguous root static paths (e.g. /jquery.min.js, /bootstrap.min.js) to dashboard target instead of tar1090.

Tunnel compatibility fix for PiAware/FR24 UI: skip JS/CSS rewrite transforms on /piaware and /fr24 paths to avoid corrupting third-party minified bundles (jquery/bootstrap regex parse errors). Keep HTML path-prefix rewrites for tunnel routing.

Tunnel proxy hardening for PiAware/FR24 UI rendering: request uncompressed upstream payloads (Accept-Encoding: identity) and add best-effort gzip decompression fallback when Content-Encoding metadata is missing, preventing garbled binary text pages.
Tunnel routing alignment: classify /fr24, /piaware, /flightaware and FR24 root assets (/logo.png, /monitor.json) as dashboard-target traffic to prevent tar1090 misrouting and feeder-side 404s.

Feeder tunnel FR24/PiAware compatibility: route /fr24 and /piaware to dashboard target, treat /flightaware as alias to /piaware, and rewrite both absolute and quoted-port link patterns (:8754/:8082) to tunneled paths without broad string replacements.

Feeder tunnel: properly route /fr24 and /flightaware paths as dashboard target so the feeder's nginx proxies them to :8754 and :8082 internally. Rewrite only full absolute URLs (http://host:8754/... and http://host:8082/...) to tunnel paths — no broad string replacements that corrupt other content.
Feeder tunnel: remove all FR24/FlightAware port-rewrite logic that was corrupting proxied HTML/JS and breaking tunnel access entirely.
Feeder tunnel: map FR24/FlightAware port-rewritten links to the feeder /feeds page instead of non-existent /fr24 and /flightaware paths.
DB compatibility: prevent startup crash on legacy databases missing feeders.device_mac by adding preflight migration before schema/index apply and retrying schema load when needed.
Startup resiliency: app now fails open if feeder tunnel routes cannot import, so core dashboard pages avoid site-wide 502 and tunnel issues remain isolated.
Feeder tunnel: rewrite dynamic JS-generated FR24/FlightAware host:port links (8754/8082) to tunnel-prefixed paths so clicks stay under /feeder/<id>/... instead of external host ports.
Feeder tunnel: tighten FR24/FlightAware URL rewrite logic to avoid malformed JS-generated links that could trigger proxy 502 responses.
Feeder tunnel: fix FR24/FlightAware 502 regression by keeping X-Tunnel-Target on supported values (dashboard/tar1090) while still rewriting :8754/:8082 links under /feeder/<id>/...
Feeder tunnel: rewrite FR24 (:8754) and FlightAware (:8082) links to stay under /feeder/<id>/..., and route those paths through dedicated tunnel targets so pages load through the proxy.

Feeder identity: beast-proxy now supports optional pre-Beast metadata line TAKNET_FEEDER_MAC <mac> and uses normalized MAC as primary upsert key (fallback to IP/hostname), preventing duplicate feeder records when public-IP feeders move to a new network.
UI: hide the sidebar mobile-toggle button by default and only show it on mobile breakpoints so desktop browsers no longer display the floating menu button.
CoT settings UX: move "Pass only TIS-B" control from output connection modal to COTProxy config page while keeping the same pass_only_tisb behavior.
Outputs guidance: move airplanes.live endpoint details out of the JSON type tile into a dedicated JSON Helper Guidance box with itemized paths and descriptions.
CoT outputs: add "Pass only TIS-B" option to pass detected TIS-B traffic in addition to COTProxy-matched aircraft while preserving existing TIS-B CoT handling.
Outputs page guidance: JSON tile in Output Types and Controls now lists airplanes.live-style private compatibility endpoints under /api/outputs/json/<key>/v2/....

JSON outputs now provide private airplanes.live-style compatibility endpoints at /api/outputs/json/<key>/v2/* (all, health, hex, callsign, reg, type, squawk, mil, ladd, pia, point) with per-output filters applied.
Outputs guidance panel: color-code output type tiles to match list badge colors (ADSB Direct, JSON, Beast RAW, CoT).
Retire legacy public /v2 REST API (returns 410) and replace Outputs page REST reference card with guidance on output types and core controls.
Outputs details modal: show generated API key section (for keyed API outputs) with copy-to-clipboard support; keeps existing ADSB Direct URL copy control.
Fix ADSB Direct URL copy button: add robust clipboard fallback for browsers/environments where navigator.clipboard is unavailable.
Outputs UI: ADSB Direct details modal now shows Save (not Save Notes) when editing a key-locked output.
Outputs UI: make ADSB Direct detail URL copyable in modal; hide raw API keys in outputs list and show key type/status only.
ADSB Direct UX: enforce durable keys, hide mode/key-type selection, and show base output URL (/api/outputs/direct/<key>/point) in output details after creation.
Outputs: add API-only ADSB Direct output type with key-in-URL endpoint (/api/outputs/direct/<key>/point/<lat>/<lon>/<radius_nm>) and per-output filters for include-network and altitude min/max.
UI: add TAKNET logo image to the left sidebar header (same asset as login page) while keeping version text.
Access request page: use POST/Redirect/GET after submit to prevent refresh re-submission; success message now tells users they will receive an approval email.
CoTProxy duplicate-rectify flow: add Skip action to move past current mismatched HEX without merging; Finish still saves current HEX and exits.
CoTProxy: add 'Find duplicates' button with modal to auto-merge exact duplicate transforms and resolve mismatched HEX groups field-by-field before merging.
CoTProxy transforms: reject duplicate HEX values per output during create/import/update, with per-row CSV skipping and clear error messages.
Feeder claiming now merges claimed username into existing feeder Owners list (deduped) instead of overwriting it; admin Lock owner list still prevents changes.

Fix dashboard 502 on upgrade: remove feeder_claim_key unique index from web/schema.sql executescript (existing DBs had no column yet). Index is still created in models.get_db() after ALTER ADD COLUMN.

Feeder claiming: permanent user Feeder claim key (Account details), optional TCP line TAKNET_FEEDER_CLAIM <uuid> before Beast on 30004 (beast-proxy); auto-sets feeder owners unless admins enable Lock owner list. Spec: FEEDER_CLAIM_CLIENT_SPEC.md.
Users: admin-only Delete on the user list (with safe username handling) and a Delete user section on user detail for other accounts; backend unchanged (POST /config/users/<id>/delete).
Email: when an admin approves a pending self-registration, send a Resend welcome email (TAKNET-PS intro + Log in button) to the user's profile email if configured.
Map: tar1090 map key modal — reorder (Filters then Search), sidebar arrow hint, drop Mobile and URL parameters sections.
Map: tar1090 map key modal — remove icon-size / zoom footer row and unused plane-icons asset.
Map: tar1090 map key modal — Buttons list order U, H, T, L, O, K, B, M, P, I, R, F.
Map: tar1090 map key modal — Location & range rings copy updated (geolocation + map layers / fixed ring distances).
Map: tar1090 map key modal — Buttons section lists H Home/Reset Map first (same icon as map-help).
Map: tar1090 map key modal — remove hero header image and map-layers radar figure; drop unused static assets.

Map: tar1090 map key modal restyled (teal/cream palette, Open Sans) and bundled map-help figures under static/img/tar1090-key/ (header strip, filter screenshot, toolbar icons, layers radar, altitude bar, body texture).
Map: tar1090 map key is a modal on /map (removed /map/tar1090-help page); no external map-help references in the UI.
Map: add /map/tar1090-help (tar1090 legend, filters, shortcuts, URL params) and map toolbar button linking to it; official ADS-B Exchange map help linked from the page.
Map: tar1090 embed layout — banner is in normal flow above the iframe so it no longer covers the top of the map.
CoT: update unknown-air MIL-STD type to use affiliation=unknown (a-u-A-U) instead of affiliation=friendly (a-f-A-U) for TIS-B markers.
Diagnostics: aircraft lookup now normalizes hex queries by extracting 6 hex digits (so '~29AD8E' matches aircraft.json even when the map display includes a prefix).
CoT: detect TIS-B via text squawk (e.g. "TIS-B") and force MIL-STD-2525 unknown air type; suppress numeric squawk remark duplication.
CoT: preserve existing COTProxy config when editing output data filters so optional settings (pass_all, include icon, etc.) do not reset.
CoT: distress emergency decoding now distinguishes emergency codes (none/general/medical/nordo/downed/emergency/reserved) and adds emergency type into remarks.
Diagnostics: auto-run CoT output preview from the aircraft query (no Inspect buttons) and improve Copy JSON clipboard reliability.
CoTProxy: distress criteria now include squawks 7700/7600/7500 and remarks include the matching distress descriptor.
Map: default view is now tar1090.
CoTProxy: distress aircraft now override COTProxy-provided CoT type (forced hostile) regardless of transform rules.
CoTProxy: prefix distress aircraft callsign with '*ALERT* - '.
CoTProxy: add Distress -> Hostile toggle (emergency status or squawk 7700 forces hostile CoT variant).
CoT: force COTProxy enabled for all CoT outputs and remove COTProxy selector from output setup modal.
UI: Users table includes First/Last name columns with sort + search, plus role dropdown filtering (feeder-list style sorting).
UI: make Users table sortable and filterable by Username and Role.
UI: show Pending Access Requests section even when there are zero pending users.
Auth: request-access signup now supports user-controlled password unmasking via Show password checkbox.
Auth: harden request-access signup password inputs to remain masked even if client-side type toggles occur.
Auth: registration alert email links to the Users list (/config/users) and new pending requests default to network_admin.
Config: add Clean user database button to purge denied/rejected users (free usernames).
Auth: purge rejected users (delete row) so their usernames can be re-registered.

Config: Resend settings now include a multi-email field for New/Pending user notification recipients (stored in RESEND_ADMIN_EMAILS) and registration notifications no longer depend on admin profile email.
Auth: registration admin notification now uses RESEND_ADMIN_EMAILS (fallback to admin profile emails) so default seeded admins without emails still receive alerts.
Auth: notify active admins via Resend email when a new user submits a registration request.
Auth: add password reset email flow via Resend (forgot/reset token routes + templates).
Users: show permission group as read-only for non-admins on their User Details page.
Auth: new access requests no longer ask users to pick a permission group; admin sets role during approval.
Auth: access requests now require user profile fields (name, email, phone, agency) and a requested permission group.
Users: admin can edit profile fields and permission group from the User Details screen; user menu links to self details.
Users: add per-user details view (username, profile fields) and admin reset-password action; user list links to details and the top-right user menu opens details for the current account.
RBAC: Diagnostics pages and API endpoints are available to network administrators as well as admins.
Diagnostics: add Output Inspector to preview CoT XML/transform output for a searched aircraft hex/callsign.
Diagnostics: add an admin Input Feed Inspector page to fetch and display the raw `aircraft.json` record for a specific hex/callsign.
CoT remarks: include aircraft `hex` in the <detail><remarks> text for easier debugging and transform validation.
CoT classification: improve ADS-B emitter category parsing (handle float-like and short hex strings) so lighter-than-air aircraft classify as civ/mil LTA instead of civil generic.

Feeder owners: JSON list of usernames per feeder (admin-only edit). Non-admins only see/list/tunnel feeders they own; empty owners = admin-only. Tunnel /feeder/<id>/ requires a matching dashboard feeder + owner (except admin). See docs/FEEDER_OWNERS.md.
Feeder tunnel: if proxy returns feeder offline (503), retry with dash/underscore variants of feeder_id so aggregator URLs (e.g. tn_hcso_adsb) match tunnel register ids (e.g. tn-hcso-adsb).
Feeder detail: replace Connect helper text with note that feeder web interface opens in a new window.

Feeder tunnel JS rewrite fix: remove early return when bundle already contains /feeder/<id> so remaining absolute /api paths (including /api/dashboard/bootstrap) are still rewritten under feeder prefix.

Feeder tunnel compatibility: keep static passthrough only for tar1090 target; re-enable dashboard JS/CSS rewrites so feeder dashboard API calls are prefixed under /feeder/<id>/ and avoid /api/dashboard/bootstrap 404 on aggregator root.
Feeder tunnel latency optimization: reuse pooled HTTP session for tunnel calls and cache feeder host lookup for 60s to reduce per-request overhead on dashboard/map/stats asset loads.
Feeder tunnel performance: passthrough static tar1090/graphs assets without rewrite/decompression, add cache-control for tunnel static assets, and keep rewrites scoped to non-static pages.
Feeder tunnel map fallback correction: missing upintheair.json now returns {"rings":[]} to match tar1090 drawUpintheair() expectations.
Feeder tunnel map fallback: change missing upintheair.json response to empty JSON array [] (instead of object) to match tar1090 drawUpintheair expectations.
Feeder tunnel map fallback: return {"upintheair":[]} for missing upintheair.json so tar1090 drawUpintheair() does not crash.
Feeder tunnel map polish: return empty JSON for missing upintheair.json endpoints to avoid non-fatal tar1090 console 404 noise.
Feeder tunnel tar1090/graphs follow-up: map /upintheair.json to /data/upintheair.json and map /graphs/* requests from graphs1090 pages to /graphs1090/graphs/* to resolve remaining image/data 404s.
Feeder tunnel graphs fix: when referer is /graphs1090/, rewrite root-level asset paths (/portal.css, /graphs.js, etc.) to /graphs1090/<asset> before proxying to feeder.
Feeder tunnel map/stats stability: stop JS/CSS rewriting for tar1090 target to prevent minified JS corruption; inject base href only for tar1090/graphs HTML.

Feeder tunnel map/stats fix: resolve relative assets under current subpath (base href uses /graphs1090/ when applicable) and route tar1090 asset paths (libs/images/root CSS/JS) to tar1090 backend via X-Tunnel-Target hints.
Feeder tunnel: open Connect at /dashboard (so / can map to tar1090), add X-Tunnel-Target header hints (dashboard/tar1090) for feeder-side backend dispatch, and document routing hints for map/stats.
CoT push: default CoT stale seconds to 30 (was 15) for COTProxy config field and pipeline fallback.
Feeders: change stale detection to use recent messages-per-second (via snapshots in settings) so active list reflects real ADS-B traffic rather than VPN/NetBird state.
Feeder detail: remove Map/Stats buttons that do not work reliably over tunnel and add note on Connect to feeder explaining map/stats require direct VPN/NetBird access.
Tunnel: add GET /feeders to list registered feeder_id and host for debugging (see docs/FEEDER_TUNNEL_REGISTER.md).

Feeder tunnel: try alternate feeder_id forms (dashes/underscores) when resolving host from tunnel; normalize host (strip scheme, add :8080 if no port) so map/stats work when feeder registers with different id or URL-style host.
Feeder tunnel: feeder can send 'host' (e.g. '100.85.149.249:8080') in register; aggregator uses it for Host header so map/stats work. See docs/FEEDER_TUNNEL_REGISTER.md.
Feeder tunnel: use origin without trailing slash in JS so map/stats URLs avoid .../id//graphs1090 (404). Host fallback to localhost:8080 when feeder has no IP.

Feeder tunnel: set Host to feeder IP:8080 when proxying so feeder serves tar1090 at / (fixes Map loading dashboard instead of map). Add Map (tar1090) and Stats (graphs1090) links on feeder detail page.

Feeder tunnel proxy: replace window.location.origin in JS (inline and external) with proxy base URL so feeder map/stats links (built from origin) resolve under /feeder/<id>/ and no longer show 'no url' or wrong page.
Feeder tunnel proxy: rewrite any http(s)://host:8080 (e.g. feeder NetBird IP) to proxy path so Map and Statistics links work when feeder uses non-localhost URL.
Feeder tunnel proxy: fix HTML rewrite so href/src/url replacements do not inject extra quote (use path-only replacement); fixes Unexpected token '<' and partial page load.
Fix 502 on aggregator: correct unterminated f-strings in feeder_tunnel HTML rewrite (href/src/url replacement values) so dashboard app starts.
Feeder tunnel proxy: rewrite feeder local URLs (127.0.0.1:8080, localhost:8080) to proxy path so Map and Statistics links open through tunnel instead of failing.

Feeder tunnel proxy: skip rewriting "'/" when it's part of a regex literal (e.g. /'/g for Settings WiFi SSID escaping) so Update/NetBird sections load; defensive fix before feeder removes path-like regex.

Feeder tunnel proxy: stop rewriting inside regex literals; only rewrite paths in quoted strings and after + to fix 'Invalid regular expression flags' and restore Update/NetBird sections on feeder Settings.
Feeder tunnel proxy: rewrite inline <script> in HTML so fetch("/api/...") in feeder dashboard page goes through proxy; fixes API 404s when calls are in inline scripts.
Feeder tunnel proxy: rewrite JS template literals and all "/api/" paths so feeder dashboard API calls (power-status, taknet-ps/stats, network-quality, sdr/status, etc.) go through proxy and work.

Feeder tunnel proxy: decompress gzip/deflate/br before rewriting HTML/JS/CSS and drop Content-Encoding so browser gets plain body; fixes ERR_CONTENT_DECODING_FAILED when feeder sends compressed responses.

Feeder tunnel proxy: rewrite HTML (inject <base>, prefix href/src), JS and CSS so feeder dashboard assets and API calls (style.css, dashboard.js, /api/power-status, etc.) load via /feeder/<id>/ and no longer 404.
Feeder tunnel: keep underscores in tunnel_feeder_id so aggregator URL matches feeder register id (e.g. 92882-test_test_test); fix 'not connected via tunnel' when feeder was connected.
Active feeder tracking: beast-proxy now closes connections that receive no data for INACTIVE_FEEDER_TIMEOUT (default 120s). Physically offline feeders are marked offline instead of staying active.
Feeders: 'Connect to feeder' button on feeder detail and list opens feeder web UI via tunnel (/feeder/<tunnel_feeder_id>/). Visible only to admin and network_admin; viewers do not see the button.

Feeder tunnel: WebSocket server at /tunnel for outbound feeder connections; reverse proxy at /feeder/<feeder_id>/ to access feeder web UIs by URL without VPN. Login required for /feeder/; Location/Content-Location rewritten to stay under proxy path.
VPN page: preserve open/closed state of Tailscale and NetBird sections on refresh.
Feeders page: default status filter to Active.
COTProxy: Evenly space icons in Civil, Military, PS Air reference tables using CSS Grid.
COTProxy: Remove descriptive paragraphs from Civil, Military, and PS Air reference sections.

COTProxy: Removed NATO Friend Air from reference and selector; Civil/Military icons now use custom icons from cot_ref_icons (civ/mil generic, fixed wing, rotary, LTA, UAV); Unknown air (TIS-B) uses Unknown air.png.
COTProxy: Civil and Military reference sections overhauled — now use icon grids (same layout as PS Air / NATO Icons); clickable to pick icon + CoT type; removed milsymbol dependency.
COTProxy: NATO Icons (Friend Air) reference section — grid of 104 icons like PS Air Icons; clickable to pick for transform; added to modal icon picker; iconUrlForPath resolves Friend Air paths.
COTProxy: Civil and Military CoT reference tables now use NATO Friend Air icons (from NATO Icons zip) instead of milsymbol; Military UAV falls back to milsymbol.
Feeders: also parse MLAT client name on '___v' (e.g. 92563-Leckliter___v2.59.34) in addition to ' | v' for software version.
Feeders: parse MLAT client name on ' | v' — display name (before) and software version (after) shown on feeder list, feeder detail, and NetBird peers table (version by IP match).
COTProxy: CoT type reference tables show 2525 symbol PNGs via milsymbol library; link to sidc.milsymb.net for reference.
COTProxy: expandable CoT type reference sections — Civil aircraft and Military aircraft (MIL-STD-2525D), code + name tables matching CoT type dropdown; link to FTS CoT table.
COTProxy: PS Air Icons — remove path-format code text; show icon names as readable (underscores to spaces, no .png); full label visible with wrap.
CoT: TIS-B specialized handling — TIS-B aircraft (readsb type tisb_*) get unknown air track (a-f-A-U) and remarks include Squawk: TISB_B.
User menu: High contrast view toggle — light theme with dark text for bright conditions/glare; preference stored in localStorage and applied across pages.
Outputs: fix network admin unable to edit/delete own connection — compare created_by to user_id as int so Flask-Login string id matches DB; users can always view, edit, delete their own outputs.

COTProxy transform rules table: column filters — freetype (HEX, callsign, reg, model) filter as you type; dropdowns (type, domain, agency, CoT type) filter on selection; facets API for dropdown options; Clear link to reset filters.
COTProxy transforms: CoT type is a dropdown of 2525c/d presets (common names); table shows common name (and code on hover); custom CoT string still available via Custom option.
Range API: fix 404 for path-based URL (use string path segments and parse to float so negative longitude works); add GET /api/outputs/range?lat=&lon=&radius_nm=&key= as alternative.

CoT push: connection reuse (persistent socket per output, reconnect on failure), delta updates (send only aircraft with changed lat/lon/alt/track/gs), configurable interval via COT_PUSH_INTERVAL_SECONDS (default 2), shorter aircraft fetch timeout; goal 1–2s marker update rate.
CoT push: interval increased from 2s to 5s so each cycle has time to complete when sending large marker counts (e.g. 3000); avoids skipped runs and allows full batch per cycle.

CoT push: configurable CoT stale time per output (cot_stale_seconds, 5–300s) and default reduced to 15s so TAK markers refresh/expire sooner; add CoT stale (seconds) control on COTProxy config page to reduce slow-update feel in TAK.

CoT push: optimize for large marker counts (e.g. 3000): bulk-load transforms per output (one DB query instead of N), precompute time/stale once per cycle, batch socket send (single buffer), adaptive send timeout for large batches so 2s update interval can be sustained.

CoT push: derive type description from ICAO type (t) when feed has no t_adsb/type_desc/desc; use web/data/icao_type_to_desc_first.json (tar1090-db) so fixed/rotor CoT types apply when readsb provides t (e.g. B738, A109).
CoT push: fix TypeError when aircraft has no category (cat None); add defensive checks and try/except so CoT flow is not interrupted by bad or missing data.

CoT push: use tar1090/ADS-B Exchange type description when present (t_adsb, type_desc, or desc 3–4 char: L/S/A/T=fixed, H/G=rotor) to set CoT type; falls back to emitter category. See ADS-B Exchange map help and FTS CoT table.
CoT push: untransformed CoT type derived from ADS-B per FTS CoT table — civil/military from dbFlags (bit 1), fixed/rotor/LTA/UAV from ICAO emitter category (e.g. a-f-A-C-F, a-f-A-M-H).
CoT push: remarks on all sent CoT now include source (taknet-ps), feed type (ADSBHub or direct feed), and CoT-Proxy when transformed; then transform remarks or squawk/category as before.

CoT push: untransformed aircraft use MIL-STD-2525 type a-f-A (friendly air). Add ADS-B-derived detail: remarks with squawk/category when no transform; track slope (climb angle) from baro_rate when gs present. Ensures adsbcot-style values from network data.

CoT push: add video URL to transforms. Transform modal has Video URL field; when set, CoT includes <__video url="..."/> on event root (COTProxy parity). Table has Video column (Yes/No). CSV import supports VIDEO.

CoT push: full COTProxy/adsbcot parity. Send detail@callsign; contact with optional name (from transform reg); <remarks> when transform has remarks; <track speed="..." course="..."/> from aircraft gs/track when available. Added remarks column to cot_transforms and Remarks field in COTProxy UI; CSV import supports REMARKS.

CoT push: add option to omit icon from CoT. 'Include transform icon in CoT' toggle on COTProxy config (default on). Uncheck to stop sending <usericon> so ATAK label sits at normal height when custom icon caused label to appear too high.
CoT push: include transform icon in CoT message. When a COTProxy transform has an icon set, add <usericon iconsetpath="..."/> under <detail> so ATAK/TAK displays the chosen icon.
COTProxy transform rules: paginated table (50/100/200/500 per page), page navigation, sortable columns (Hex, Callsign, Type, Domain, Agency, Reg). GET single transform for edit modal.
COTProxy: bulk-delete no longer times out on large transform lists. Single-connection chunked DELETE (500 per batch); add delete_all option and 'Delete all' button for clearing without sending IDs.

JSON output: add Range API mode. When JSON is selected, choose Stream or Range API; Range API uses center & radius in URL (GET /api/outputs/range/point/<lat>/<lon>/<radius_nm>?key=...). Respects Include Network ADSB; no range/elevation in modal for Range API.

JSON output: fix 'Include Network ADSB' unchecked still showing all traffic. Normalize include_network_adsb to bool in dashboard stream and taknet-api (handle string/config from DB); parse config as string when needed.
CoT format: use vertical accuracy for point 'le' (50m or 9999999 unknown) per PyTAK/adsbcot/node-cot; was incorrectly set to altitude. Compliance doc updated.

JSON output 'Include Network ADSB' unchecked: dashboard stream and v2 API now both filter to direct feeder only (exclude source=adsbhub). Dashboard stream config parsing fixed for dict/string; api-server loads output config and applies filter for key-authenticated v2 requests.

CoT sender: step-by-step INFO logging (cycle start, fetch, connect, TLS, send, done); job returns immediately + single-run lock; shorter timeouts (2+3s fetch, 3s socket) for diagnosis and TAK connectivity.
CoT sender: run in thread with 12s cap so scheduler never blocks; aircraft fetch via requests with (3,5)s timeout to avoid DNS/connect hang; fixes 'max instances' and no CoT.
CoT sender: interval 3s → 10s, socket timeout 10s → 5s to prevent job overlap and 'maximum instances reached' skips.
Outputs: enable/disable per output — switch in each output's settings (modal) and On/Off button in the outputs list. Disabled outputs stop push/API/CoT until re-enabled.
CoT push TLS: accept self-signed TAK Server certificates (disable server cert verification) so connection succeeds to typical TAK Server deployments.
CoT push: in-app sender runs every 3s — fetches aircraft, applies COTProxy transforms and pass_all filter, builds CoT XML, pushes over TCP/TLS to TAK Server. No external adsbcot required.
P12 upload: handle cryptography ValueError('Invalid password or PKCS12 data') — retry with empty bytes when no password given and show friendly error message.
P12 upload: support password-less .p12 files; use None for no password and fallback to empty bytes if needed; clearer error when password is wrong.
CoT push TLS: upload .p12/.pfx file; backend extracts certificate and key. Optional password field for protected P12.
CoT push: Connection dropdown (TCP/TLS), server address and port fields with defaults 8087/8089. Certs section only for TLS; clearer cert/key copy and optional CA removed.
Include Network ADSB Traffic: now enforced for JSON output stream (excludes aircraft with source=adsbhub when unchecked). UI note: Beast Raw cannot filter by source.
Output option: Include Network ADSB Traffic checkbox. When checked, network (e.g. ADSBHub) traffic included; when unchecked, only direct feeder traffic.
Output range filter placeholders: DD.DDDD, -DDD.DDDD, RANGE in NM.

Output data filters: Range Limits (lat/lon/radius nm, max 250 nm) and Elevation Filter (min/max ft MSL, 0–40,000) in output setup modal. Apply to JSON, Beast Raw, and CoT. Both off by default; enable checkboxes show/hide sections. Elevation has No Minimum/No Maximum and paired sliders (100 ft steps 0–5000, 1000 ft above).
COTProxy: Pass all traffic toggle (default off). Off = only transformed aircraft sent; on = all traffic with transforms applied where defined.
CoT push: PyTAK-compliant protocol documented (COT_PUSH_COMPLIANCE.md). cot_pipeline documents URL/XML/framing/TLS and COT_MESSAGE_DELIMITER for senders.
COTProxy bulk delete: modal during removal (blocks navigation); on success auto-dismisses after 1.5s; on error user dismisses.
COTProxy CSV import: use single DB transaction for bulk insert to avoid 504 Gateway Time-out on large uploads.
CoT push TLS certs: upload section disabled (unselectable) until connection is saved; then enabled for upload or replace.
CoT push TLS certs: upload form always visible in output modal; after creating new CoT push output modal stays open to upload certs in same window; Save becomes Close.

COTProxy config: CSV import shows processing modal (blocks navigation), then success or failure with Dismiss. Bulk delete: checkboxes in first column, Select all (top/header/bottom), Delete selected at top and bottom; API bulk-delete.

CoT push: TLS client certificate upload in output modal (same window). Certs stored encrypted; only the output owner can upload or replace; cert content never returned to UI or admins. Owner sees only 'Certificate installed' or upload form; can replace without viewing.
Outputs: relabel format options to JSON, Beast RAW, CoT.
COTProxy config: PS Air Icons (Public Safety Air v8) included as reference; icon picker in Add/Edit transform and collapsible reference section.
Transform table shows actual icon thumbnail when a PS Air icon path is selected.
PyTAK (https://github.com/snstac/pytak) referenced for TAK/CoT protocol handling in UI and cot_pipeline.
Output format: network admins can choose As-is (JSON/Beast Raw) or Cursor on Target (CoT).
CoT outputs support optional COTProxy-style transforms (callsign, type, icon per ICAO hex).
COTProxy config page per CoT output: manual transform table, CSV bulk import, downloadable template (DOMAIN,AGENCY,REG,CALLSIGN,TYPE,MODEL,HEX,COT,ICON).
API: /api/outputs/<id>/cot-transforms (CRUD), /api/outputs/<id>/cot-transforms/import, /api/outputs/cot-transforms/template.
cot_pipeline module for future adsbcot/COTProxy integration (get_cot_push_outputs, get_transform_for_aircraft).
Outputs page: added sample API URL (adsb.tak-solutions.com) and curl example for REST API (v2).
Outputs page: added REST API (v2) reference card with endpoints table and link to airplanes.live API guide.
Removed Airplanes.live incoming feed; merger and UI reverted to local + ADSBHub only (equivalent to v1.0.124 behavior).
Airplanes.live: 429 back off reduced to 10s (from 60s) for faster recovery when rate limited.
Fix: Airplanes.live rate limit (429) — default poll interval 5s to stay under 1 req/sec; on 429 back off 60s and keep last data instead of clearing map.
Fix: Airplanes.live — parse API response using "ac" key (airplanes.live returns aircraft array as "ac", not "aircraft"). Receive and map data now connect correctly.

Feature: Airplanes.live incoming — receive merged aircraft from airplanes.live API (point/radius). Config → Services: toggle Receive from Airplanes.live; dashboard status card; feeder data remains primary when duplicates exist; 10s staleness; data cleared when off.
Docs: ADSBHUB_INTEGRATION.md — add Verification section (receive off discards ADSBHub data; 10s staleness for local and ADSBHub; manual checks).
Docs: document ADSBHUB_STATUS_DIR in env.example and ADSBHUB_INTEGRATION.md (feeder and merger status file path; used by dashboard for feed/receive connection status).

ADSBHub receive off: map and API clear immediately — dashboard writes receive_enabled to shared volume; merger reads each cycle and drops all ADSBHub aircraft when disabled. Staleness: aircraft not seen in 10s (MERGER_STALE_SECONDS) are purged from merged output so map/API get no stale data. Dashboard adsbhub-status volume rw for receive_enabled file.
Dashboard: Aircraft Tracked card shows breakdown — direct (feeders) and network (ADSBHub); API status returns aircraft.direct and aircraft.network from merger source field.

Map: fix aircraft marker click — map click no longer clears sidebar when clicking a dot; larger hit area (radius 8); L.DomEvent.stopPropagation and ev.target for reliable sidebar details. REST API docs: document merger-added fields source and on_ground.

Map: Merged view is default; tar1090 iframe loads only when you switch to tar1090 (avoids 404s in console). Clear hint: 'Click a blue dot → details in sidebar' and banner on tar1090 view: 'For aircraft detail sidebar, switch to Merged (all) above.'
Fix: Merged map sidebar — stop click propagation on aircraft marker so map click does not clear selection; invalidateSize when showing Merged view; use marker._ac for sidebar
Merged map: tar1090-style sidebar — click aircraft for details (ICAO, callsign, altitude, speed, track, vert rate, squawk, position, source, on ground); click map to clear
ADSBHub merger: parse on_ground from MSG,3; ADSBHUB_INTEGRATION.md documents all fields from feed
Fix: Merged map fetches /api/aircraft.json (dashboard proxy to merger) so map works when /data/aircraft.json returns 404 (e.g. nginx not routing)
Map: default view is 'Merged (all)' — Leaflet map fetches /data/aircraft.json so all aircraft (local + ADSBHub) show; toggle to tar1090 for full UI
Nginx: /tar1090/data/aircraft.json prefix match, pass query string, no-cache headers
Fix: tar1090 map now uses merged aircraft (local + ADSBHub) — nginx routes /tar1090/data/aircraft.json to aircraft-merger so map shows all positions
ADSBHub receive: aircraft-merger now merges SBS messages by ICAO (MSG,1 + MSG,3 + MSG,4) so ADSBHub aircraft show position on map instead of only callsign/velocity
Fix: dashboard container start failure — mount adsbhub-status at /app/adsbhub-status (not under read-only /app/var); set ADSBHUB_STATUS_PATH for dashboard
Docs: escape $ in ADSBHUB_CLIENT_KEY as $$ in .env when using Docker Compose
ADSBHub: Config → Services — Feed/Receive toggles plus CLIENTKEY field; key and toggles persisted to .env and feeder/merger restarted on Save
ADSBHub feed: SBS from readsb:30003, CLIENTKEY sent as first line to data.adsbhub.org:5001 (per Connection Guide); ADSBHUB_CLIENT_KEY in env and compose

ADSBHub.org TCP integration: feed Beast to data.adsbhub.org:5001 (adsbhub-feeder); receive SBS from :5002 and merge with local (aircraft-merger); prefer local per ICAO for accuracy; ADSBHUB_INTEGRATION.md
Health: capacity calculator shows est. more feeders by type — NetBird, Tailscale, Public (VPN ~2×/1.5× CPU per feeder)
Health: background refresh no longer flashes screen — data updates in place every 5s without showing loading overlay
Health: Capacity calculator — CPU % per feeder, target %, remaining %, est. more feeders; feeder breakdown by NetBird/Tailscale/Public; conservative estimate when adding VPN feeders
Health: show active feeder count in Overview and capacity-planning note (baseline/peak CPU vs feeder count; keep peak under ~70–80%); README capacity planning subsection
Health: Top processes show MEM %, RSS (MB), Status like top; host snapshot collects memory_percent, rss_mb, status; page refreshes every 5s while viewing
Fix: Host health snapshot now reports real process CPU % — use two samples with 0.2s sleep so psutil returns non-zero values (first call per process was 0)

Host health snapshot fully automated: installer restarts dashboard after enabling timer so no manual step; .env gets INSTALL_DIR if missing for var mount; Top Processes note shortened to 'set up automatically during install/update'

Installer: fix host psutil install — try dnf/yum python3-psutil first, then install gcc and python3-devel before pip; clearer warning with exact fix (dnf install gcc python3-devel && pip3 install psutil)

Health (entire VM): Top Processes card titled 'entire VM, by CPU' with clear enable steps when host snapshot off; dashboard var mount uses INSTALL_DIR from .env; installer substitutes INSTALL_DIR into systemd service for non-default install paths

New: Config → Health shows full server view when host snapshot is enabled — overview, history chart and top processes (netbird, readsb, python, etc.) from the host; installer runs scripts/health_snapshot_host.py every 30s via systemd timer and dashboard reads var/health_history.json (mount /opt/taknet-aggregator/var); requires python3 and psutil on host

New: Health history on Config → Health — CPU-over-time chart for the last 60 minutes (30s samples); hover over the chart to see top processes at that time to identify what causes CPU spikes (e.g. 30% vs normal 10%)

New: System Health page (Config → Health) — detailed CPU per-core, memory breakdown, disk per-mount, and top processes (like top/htop); dashboard System Health card links to it for admins; GET /api/health/detail (admin only)

Fix: Update check now bypasses cache — API sends no-store headers and requests GitHub with no-cache so 'Check Now' and page load always see latest; Updates page fetch uses cache: 'no-store' so browser does not reuse cached 'no update available'
Change: Outputs table — removed Status column, added Created column showing date/time each output was created
Fix: Dashboard now reads VERSION from the same host file used by taknet-agg and the update system, ensuring the web UI 'Installed' version stays in sync with CLI status and GitHub releases
Fix: docker-compose mounts /opt/taknet-aggregator/VERSION into the dashboard container at /app/VERSION so /api/updates/check and the sidebar version badge always reflect the current installed release
New: REST_API_DOCUMENTATION.md reference for all /v2 REST endpoints with curl examples to help new integrators onboard quickly
Change: Removed dashboard 'Recent Activity' card to declutter the main view while keeping feeder breakdown and system health front and center
Fix: install.sh no longer removes netbird-server or netbird-dashboard during cleanup — those are managed by a separate compose project and must not be touched during taknet updates
Fix: remove duplicate taknet-api service in docker-compose.yml that caused container name conflict preventing startup
Fix: install.sh correctly detects curl-pipe installs (BASH_SOURCE[0] empty) — now uses /opt/taknet-aggregator if it exists, clones from GitHub otherwise
Fix: container cleanup list now includes netbird-server and netbird-dashboard
Fix: install.sh now force-removes all known taknet-* containers by name before docker compose up — eliminates container name conflict errors regardless of project label state
Fix: install.sh detects and removes containers with wrong Docker Compose project labels before starting
Fix: taknet-agg update reinstalls CLI from updated install.sh before running compose, making all future fixes self-propagating
Fix: pin Docker Compose project name to 'taknet-aggregator' in docker-compose.yml — prevents container ownership conflicts when compose runs from different working directories
Fix: update script removes orphaned taknet-api container before bring-up if project label mismatch
Fix: update script now builds taknet-api image alongside other local services — resolves container name conflict on update
New: ADS-B REST API (v2) — airplanes.live-compatible endpoints at /v2/
Endpoints: /v2/all, /v2/hex/<hex>, /v2/callsign/<cs>, /v2/reg/<reg>, /v2/type/<type>, /v2/squawk/<sq>, /v2/mil, /v2/ladd, /v2/pia, /v2/point/<lat>/<lon>/<radius_nm>
REST API is public (no auth required), supports comma-separated multi-value lookups
Point endpoint returns aircraft sorted nearest-first with 'dst' (nm) field, capped at 250 nm
Durable API keys: no connection-count limits, key stays 'ready' indefinitely — regenerate to rotate
Single-use keys unchanged: consumed on first beast connection, unlimited REST API calls
Key type selector added to output creation UI

Fixed update modal showing JSON parse error after restart. When the container restarts, nginx may send an HTML error page as the last SSE chunk — JSON.parse threw and surfaced the error to the user. Fix: wrapped SSE message parsing in try/catch, silently discarding unparseable frames. Also: page now auto-reloads 1 second after the server comes back online, so manual refresh is no longer needed.

Fixed feeders going stale despite active connections. stats_flusher was crashing after its first cycle with 'Cannot operate on a closed database'. Root cause: _get_conn() returns a cached thread-local connection, but several db functions (validate_output_key, purge_old_feeders, etc.) called conn.close() on it. After close, the cached reference remains but is unusable, causing the next db call in the same thread to fail. stats_flusher task died silently, stopping all last_seen updates. Fix: removed all conn.close() calls from beast-proxy db.py — thread-local connections are long-lived by design and should not be closed.

Fixed /api/ routes returning 404 when accessed via adsb.tak-solutions.com. aggregator.conf used server_name _ (catch-all) while netbird.conf had an explicit server_name — nginx prioritizes explicit names, so adsb.tak-solutions.com was being routed to the netbird block which has no /api/ routes. Fix: added adsb.tak-solutions.com explicitly to aggregator.conf server_name.

Fixed feeder count dropping to 0 after service restart. mark_inactive_feeders was marking feeders stale based purely on active_connections snapshot — if a feeder hadn't reconnected yet when the stats cycle ran, it got wrongly marked stale. Fix: only mark a feeder stale if it is both absent from active_connections AND hasn't been seen in the last 5 minutes. This gives feeders a safe reconnection window after restart without requiring a separate grace period counter.

Reverted READSB_LAT/LON added in v1.0.80. A fixed reference point is wrong for a distributed multi-feeder aggregator — it biases local CPR decoding toward one location, causing failures for aircraft far from that point. Without lat/lon, readsb uses global CPR decoding exclusively (requires even+odd frame pair), which works correctly across the entire USA when multiple feeders are providing data.

Fixed missing distant aircraft on aggregator map. Readsb had no READSB_LAT/LON set, causing it to use 0,0 (Gulf of Guinea) as the CPR decoding reference point. CPR position decoding requires a reference location close to the aircraft — without it, positions for aircraft far from 0,0 fail to decode correctly. Now passes SITE_LAT/SITE_LON from .env into the readsb container.

Fixed all feeders showing as stale. The 24h auto-purge introduced in v1.0.76 was deleting active feeders whose last_seen hadn't been flushed yet. On the next cycle, upsert_feeder recreated them with new IDs, but active_connections held the old IDs, causing mark_inactive_feeders to immediately mark the new records stale. Fix: purge now excludes any feeder ID currently in active_connections.

Dashboard outputs stat card now shows live count from /api/outputs instead of hardcoded 0 with 'coming soon'. Count is user-scoped: network_admins see only their own outputs, admins see all — matching the outputs page behavior.

Fixed updater not applying code changes — dashboard/beast-proxy/mlat-server are locally built images that docker compose pull skips. Update script now explicitly runs 'docker compose build' for these services before bringing containers up, ensuring new code is always applied.

Feeder list auto-cleanup: beast-proxy now purges feeders not seen in 24 hours on every stats cycle. Added 'Reset Feeder List' button (admin only) to the feeder list filter bar that removes all non-active (stale/offline) feeders on demand.

Output API key modal now shows beginner-friendly connection instructions with the full URL/host (adsb.tak-solutions.com) and step-by-step guidance for both beast raw TCP and JSON HTTP. Edit modal now locks name/type/mode fields once an API key has been generated — only notes remains editable. Button changes to 'Save Notes' to reflect this.

Fixed update modal — gunicorn was running 2 worker processes, so the update thread (Worker A) wrote to an in-memory queue that the SSE stream (Worker B) could never see. Reduced to 1 worker — the dashboard is a single-admin tool and doesn't need multiple workers.

Fixed misleading API key modal text — key is visible in the outputs list, not one-time only. Deleting an output now emits a drop signal before deletion, terminating any active beast connection using that output's key.

Fixed update modal showing 'Connection lost unexpectedly' even when update succeeded. Three-part fix: (1) Added 3s sleep in shell script between PRE_RESTART signal and docker compose up, giving browser time to receive the event; (2) Added SSE padding comments after pre_restart to force gunicorn to flush the chunk immediately; (3) Added 500ms grace period in JS onerror handler to handle race between pre_restart processing and connection drop. Also removed --build flag from docker compose up since pull already fetches updated images.

Output API keys are now single-use per session: key is consumed on first successful beast auth, marked 'used', and rejected for any subsequent connections. Key and status (ready/used) are visible in the outputs list. Regenerating a key drops any active beast connection using the old key (via DB signal table polled by beast-proxy), deletes the old key, and issues a new 'ready' key. Key display stored in DB and shown persistently in the UI.

Fixed update error 'unhashable type: dict' — docker volumes dict was incorrectly embedded inside the f-string using {{ }} escaping, causing Python to evaluate it as a dict literal with a dict value as a key. Moved volumes dict outside the f-string.

Built outputs system. JSON outputs: push (aggregator POSTs to your URL) or API (GET /api/outputs/stream/<key>). Beast raw outputs: push (aggregator connects outbound to host:port) or listener (clients connect to TCP port 30005, send key as first line, receive beast stream). Keys are SHA-256 hashed in DB, shown once on create with regenerate option. Multiple simultaneous connections supported on shared port 30005. Access is network_admin-scoped.

Fixed update modal showing 'Connection lost unexpectedly' instead of reconnecting gracefully. Root cause: pre_restart signal was queued before the docker:cli container started, but gunicorn buffering meant the browser didn't receive it before containers restarted. Signal is now emitted from inside the shell script right before docker compose up runs, guaranteeing the browser has received all prior log lines first.
Corrected outputs access model: viewers have no access to outputs at all. Outputs page and API are restricted to admin and network_admin only. Removed is_public concept entirely.

Built outputs access control foundation. Each network_admin can create outputs only they can see and modify. Admins see all outputs across all users with owner column. Viewers see only outputs marked public. New outputs table in DB (auto-migrated), full CRUD API with ownership enforcement, and outputs page with create/edit/delete UI.

Fixed 'From Location' name format: country is now 3-letter ISO alpha-3 (e.g. USA), state/province is full name (e.g. Tennessee), city is full name (e.g. nashville). Added pycountry library for alpha-2 to alpha-3 conversion. Example output: usa-tennessee-nashville.

Fixed feeder detail page JS broken in v1.0.63 — async function deleteFeeder() declaration was accidentally removed by a str_replace, leaving a bare return statement at script top-level that killed all subsequent function definitions (populateName, openFeederView, onProxyLoad, etc.).

Fixed feeder detail page JS silently breaking when tar1090/graphs1090 URLs contained special characters (e.g. & in query strings). Raw Jinja URL values inside JS strings replaced with tojson filter. This also fixes the 'From Location' button appearing to do nothing.

Feeder detail page: added '📍 From Location' button that auto-populates the name field in CC-ST-City format (e.g. US-TN-nashville). Uses MLAT GPS coordinates via Nominatim reverse geocode if available, falls back to IP geolocation via ip-api.com. Name is previewed in the field; user confirms by clicking Save Changes.

Fixed duplicate feeder records when same feeder connects via both public IP and NetBird IP. beast-proxy now detects when a NetBird feeder's hostname matches an existing public-IP record and merges them on reconnect. Added 'Merge Duplicate' button on feeder detail page to manually collapse existing duplicates and re-parent their connection history.
Fixed broken web interface caused by corrupted nginx config (duplicate location blocks outside server context from a failed str_replace in v1.0.59). Config rewritten clean with single server block.

Fixed feeder proxy 404. Root cause: nginx proxy_pass with IP variables requires a resolver directive — without it nginx silently falls through to Flask which has no matching route. Added Docker DNS resolver (127.0.0.11) to nginx config. Also fixed WebSocket Connection header and simplified auth sub-request.

Fixed feeder map proxy freezing. Root cause: Flask proxy could not handle tar1090's WebSocket connections for live aircraft updates. Rewrote proxy to route through nginx (which handles WebSockets natively). Flask now only validates auth and redirects; nginx does the actual proxying to the feeder's NetBird IP with WebSocket upgrade support.

Fixed NetBird feeders being mis-classified as Tailscale. VPN resolver now checks NetBird first (primary VPN), uses a full peer list cache refreshed every 60s instead of per-IP API calls, and prefers NetBird in the CIDR fallback when both VPNs share the same range. On startup, beast-proxy now reclassifies all existing feeders against live peer lists and corrects any mis-classifications in the database.

Feeder detail page: 'View Map' and 'View Stats' buttons open the feeder's tar1090/graphs1090 in a full-screen modal proxied through the aggregator's NetBird connection, so users not on NetBird can still access feeder interfaces. Includes 'Open Directly' link for direct access when on NetBird.

NetBird connection type indicator changed from purple to orange throughout the UI (feeders page, dashboard breakdown, feeder detail). NetBird feeders now auto-populate map URL (http://IP:8080) and stats URL (http://IP:8080/graphs1090/) on first connection; existing empty fields are back-filled on reconnect.
NetBird peer table now shows peer name instead of system hostname.
Updates page now shows release notes for all versions included in an available update, displayed inside the update banner before you run it.

NetBird enrollment card: shows a green hi-vis banner when connected, hides setup key field and re-enroll button. Enrollment form only appears when not connected. Disconnect button moves into the connected banner.
VPN page peer tables now collapse by default. Click the card header to expand/collapse Tailscale or NetBird peer lists. Peer counts remain visible in the header at all times.

Added self-registration with access request workflow. New users register via 'Request Access' on the login page, enter a pending state, and see a holding screen until approved. Admins see pending requests in Users page with approve/deny/role controls, and a banner on the dashboard when requests are waiting.

Map page now fills full available height with no blank space. README rewritten: NetBird as primary VPN, Tailscale references minimized, irrelevant content removed, feeder beast_out requirement documented.
Login logo centered correctly. Services page: added Restart All Services (soft reset) button that restarts all TAKNET containers with a live status modal.
Login page logo updated to oval TAKNET-PS badge, displayed at 80% screen width.

Fixed web update — git clone and file copy now run inside docker:cli container which has the host install directory bind-mounted, ensuring files actually land on disk. Also added 1s delay before restart to ensure pre_restart event reaches the browser.
Login page restyled to match dashboard dark theme. TAKNET-PS logo added as splash above login fields.
Fixed updates page showing stale 'update available' after web update completes. Version status now refreshes automatically after the modal closes.
Removed LAT/LONG from tar1090 config. Combined with v1.0.41-42 fixes, aggregator now displays aircraft from feeders at any location without range filtering at any layer.
Removed fixed READSB_LAT/LON from readsb config. With READSB_NET_RECEIVER_ID enabled, readsb uses each feeder's own position for CPR decoding, fixing position loss for remote feeders.
Removed READSB_MAX_RANGE limit. Aggregator now accepts aircraft data from feeders at any distance, enabling nationwide and international deployments.
About page now accessible to all authenticated users (admin, network_admin, viewer).

Access control added — login required. Three roles: admin (full access), network_admin (dashboard + data), viewer (map only). Default credentials admin/password. User management page under System. Each user can change their own password via profile menu.
Web UI update button on Updates page. Live log modal streams output via SSE. Auto-reconnects after dashboard restart. Error state waits for user dismissal.
Fixed Aircraft Tracked stat card — now shows 'with position' sub-count. Fixed topbar aircraft count (was showing [object Object]).
NetBird setup key now persists to .env automatically when enrolled via the VPN page. .env mounted into dashboard container for writing.
Fixed NetBird enrollment — added /dev/net/tun device to container so WireGuard interface creates successfully.
NetBird server enrollment UI. VPN page now includes enrollment form — enter a setup key to register the aggregator as a NetBird peer. Status, re-enroll, and disconnect controls added.
NetBird self-hosted integration. Added nginx config for netbird.tak-solutions.com with TLS (Let's Encrypt). Port 443 added to nginx container with /etc/letsencrypt bind mount.
Fixed Tailscale feeder classification. Removed is_private gate that excluded CGNAT range (100.64.0.0/10) from VPN detection.
Fixed Tailscale socket API auth. Added required Sec-Tailscale header to both beast-proxy and dashboard VPN service.
Beast-proxy fetches aircraft data from tar1090 HTTP instead of non-existent readsb JSON file. Fixes 0 aircraft count. Removed unused readsb-run volume and READSB_JSON_DIR.
Added READSB_JSON_DIR to enable aircraft.json output. Fixes 0 aircraft count in dashboard and beast-proxy stats.
Updates page with version check against GitHub, release notes display, and update history log. taknet-agg update now logs to database.
Feeder names auto-populated from MLAT handshake data, overriding default IP-based naming.
Web UI now displays feeder coordinates, altitude, and MLAT status. Feeders list shows location and MLAT columns.
Beast frame parsing for accurate message/position counts. MLAT clients.json integration for coordinates, altitude, and connection status. Added altitude column to schema.
Beast protocol frame counting, position tracking, MLAT detection via shared log volume. Replaced per-flush counters with real message stats.
Time-based stats flushing every 30s. Feeders stay active while connected. Aircraft count from readsb. GeoIP enabled by default. Removed stale host mount for mmdb.
Fixed tar1090 read-only filesystem error. Replaced shared readsb volume with tmpfs mounts for tar1090's internal readsb.
Fixed mlat-server arguments for wiedehopf fork: --basestation-listen/connect instead of --results.
Added required --work-dir=/tmp argument for mlat-server.
Fixed mlat-server Dockerfile — runs script directly from cloned repo instead of pip module. Installs all Python dependencies.
GeoIP auto-download built into beast-proxy image using db-ip.com City Lite database. No registration required.
Fixed mlat-server entrypoint from binary to python3 -m. Corrected executable path resolution.
Healthcheck set to always-pass for readsb container. Resolves dependency startup failures.
Explicit healthcheck override for readsb. Removed service_healthy dependencies.
Switched all container dependencies to service_started. Removed custom healthcheck.
Changed readsb healthcheck from file check to process check. Added start_period grace.
Fixed mlat-server build: added Cython and build-essential dependencies for C extension compilation.
Custom mlat-server Dockerfile — builds from wiedehopf/mlat-server source since no published image exists.
Complete install.sh rewrite. Full validation suite: bash syntax, Python compile, YAML parse, heredoc balance.
Fixed unquoted SITE_NAME causing bash source error. Safe env variable extraction.
Renamed .env.example to env.example for macOS Finder/GitHub Desktop visibility.
Fixed dotfile deployment with shopt dotglob fallback for systems without rsync.
Added Fresh VPS Setup section to README. Installer bootstraps its own dependencies.
GitHub curl-pipe installer with auto-clone. Supports both curl-pipe and local clone workflows.
Removed parallel/cutover mode. WEB_PORT defaults to 80.
MLAT server wired into Docker stack with client and results ports.
Port corrections: Beast 30004, MLAT 30105, MLAT results 39001. Tailscale runs on host.
Detailed README with architecture overview, port map, and deployment instructions.
Initial Docker-based aggregator build. Six containers: beast-proxy, readsb, mlat-server, tar1090, dashboard, nginx.
